title: Trust And Evidence Checklist
label: Credibility opt-in
summary: A credibility checklist for source hierarchy, claim labels, corrections, association boundaries, evidence-vault navigation, and safe reading of darker public-record material.
boundary: Lead magnets are public orientation assets with a seven-day intelligence window. They do not increase evidence strength or replace trust/privacy pages.
promise: Get the source-discipline checklist before entering the darker archive routes: how to judge claim strength, source hierarchy, and association boundaries.
readerOutcome: The reader can navigate controversial material without confusing source, theory, claim, and conclusion.
whatYouLearn: The difference between primary source, public reporting, archive lead, claim, and speculation.
How corrections, privacy, and trust pages protect the reader and the site.
How to judge whether a page is making a claim or asking a research question.
Which evidence vault route to open when a number or allegation matters.
checklist: Check whether a person is central, peripheral, accused, convicted, or merely mentioned.
Use the corrections route if something needs review.
Do not share a claim stronger than the evidence route supports.
actionSteps: Use the checklist before entering darker pages.
nextBestStep: Open the Trust Center before continuing into the darker archive.
